Current (legacy) stack is .NET 8 / ASP.NET Core / Blazor WASM / EF Core / SQL Server+PostgreSQL, recorded under technology.current_stack for reference only.

Document 18 (18_DEPLOYMENT_ARCHITECTURE.md) is a supplementary deployment view added 2026-06-24; it consolidates the technology.infrastructure (TECH-INF-001..008) and deployment-relevant technology.security (TECH-SEC-010..017) nodes already present in this manifest. It introduces NO new node and NO new fact; deployment target platforms in its target-options section are NEUTRAL options, consistent with the empty target_stack mandate.

generation_priorities.rationale: Identity & Access is the cross-cutting prerequisite for Basket-transfer (BIZ-PROC-003), Order buyer-id (BR011) and admin auth. Persistence already isolated in AppIdentityDbContext (DATA-REPO-004), the cleanest cut point in the APP-DEP-001 cycle; establishes the auth contract (APP-API-001) consumed everywhere.

Catalog is upstream reference data for Basket and Order (DATA-REL-004, VO-03 snapshot). HIGHEST coupling (APP-SVC-001 score 13) and source of APP-DEP-002..007 + APP-DEP-010 violations; generated early to force resolution and to carve the shared CatalogContext (DATA-REPO-003).
Basket depends on Catalog (item references) and Identity (BuyerId soft ref DATA-REL-008) which are generated first. APP-SVC-003 coupling 9; DATA-AGG-001 boundary well-defined once BC-01/BC-04 exist.

Ordering consumes Basket (DATA-REL-011 handoff), Catalog (VO-03 snapshot) and the buyer id from Identity. LOWEST coupling among core modules (APP-SVC-004 score 4) and a clean DATA-AGG-002 boundary, but functionally downstream.

Catalog Administration is a presentation/SPA context (APP-SVC-005 score 3, APP-SVC-016 frontend) consuming BC-01 Catalog via APIs; cannot be generated before Catalog/Identity exist. OQ-001 (merge Admin+BlazorAdmin) must be decided first; runtime HTTP deps APP-DEP-017/018.

Web presentation shell + shared technical modules are composition/host concerns generated last, after domain contexts define contracts. EfRepository (coupling 16, APP-DEP-009) and the shared CatalogContext must already be split.

Buyer / Customer Profile is ASPIRATIONAL/UNIMPLEMENTED (RC-002): DATA-ENT-010/011 persisted=false, DATA-AGG-003 aspirational, payment capabilities BIZ-CAP-027/028 inferred/LOW. Generated LAST and only on an explicit forward-engineering decision; gap ASMP-FE-003.

assumptions.note: ApplicationCore, Infrastructure and SharedContracts/BlazorShared each represent one real unit appearing as both module-candidate and class-library component, and are merged (facets preserved in merged_from).

Web-hosted MVC/Razor Page routes are owned by the canonical Web service, PublicApi REST endpoints by PublicApi, and BlazorAdmin FrontendRoutes by BlazorAdmin; derived from each interface's source_file path (src/Web, src/PublicApi, src/BlazorAdmin) and owner_module, not a deployable_unit field.

Browse Catalog (BIZ-PROC-001) and Adjust Basket (BIZ-PROC-004) are treated as touching CatalogItem/CatalogBrand/CatalogType and Basket/BasketItem respectively, although they are value-stream stages rather than step-level processes; these process_to_entity links carry LOW/MEDIUM confidence.
Role ownership by Identity module inferred (confidence 0.7).

The authoritative product name is treated as 'eShopOnWeb' for labeling (project/source folder), while metadata preserves the 'unknown' caveat from source business evidence; naming only, no node semantics affected.

assumptions.statement: A Money value object (VO-05) with explicit currency is NOT derivable from evidence; Price/UnitPrice appear as bare decimals (DATA-ENT-001/005/007).
assumptions.impact: Multi-currency would be a NEW design element; default candidate is amount-only Money VO.

assumptions.statement: StockReorderTriggered (EVT-12) is inferred from OnReorder/RestockThreshold/MaxStockThreshold/AvailableStock on DATA-ENT-001; no BIZ-PROC node or business rule describes a reorder workflow.
assumptions.impact: Confirm reorder lifecycle with stakeholders before generation; weakest event candidate.

assumptions.statement: Buyer/Customer Profile context (BC-06) is surfaced only from aspirational nodes and the soft BuyerId reference; today the buyer reference is satisfied by ApplicationUser (BC-04).
assumptions.impact: Do not generate Buyer/Payment persistence without an explicit decision.

assumptions.statement: Routes physically served by the Web shell (APP-SVC-006) and the authenticate endpoint in PublicApi (APP-SVC-011) are attributed to their FUNCTIONAL bounded contexts (BC-02/BC-03/BC-04) for design; physical hosting stays with BC-07.
assumptions.impact: Distinguish functional ownership from physical hosting in downstream documents; honor OQ-009.
